address: Hat Sai Ri, Sapli, Mueang Chumphon, Chumphon 86000

description.en:
## Overview

The Prince of Chumphon Memorial Shrine is a hilltop monument overlooking Sapli Beach (Hat Sai Ri) dedicated to Admiral Prince Abhakara Kiartivongse (1880-1923), the 28th son of King Chulalongkorn (Rama V) and the man honored as the Father of the Royal Thai Navy. The prince is deeply revered throughout Chumphon province, where he spent his final years before passing away in 1923. His shrine is one of 217 memorials built across Thailand in his honor, but this one at Hat Sai Ri holds special significance as it overlooks the sea he devoted his life to.

## The Shrine and Museum

The main shrine features a commanding statue of the prince in naval uniform, positioned on a base designed in the form of a naval ship, symbolizing his role in establishing Thailand's modern navy. The complex includes a small but informative museum displaying the prince's biography, personal belongings, photographs, naval artifacts, and items related to his military career. A large torpedo ship named "Royal Chumphon" is displayed on the grounds. The shrine faces the sea, and the hilltop setting provides panoramic views of the Gulf of Thailand coastline and Sapli Beach stretching below.

## Significance

Prince Abhakara is credited with modernizing Thailand's naval forces in the early 20th century, establishing training programs and acquiring modern vessels that formed the foundation of today's Royal Thai Navy. The Thai Navy marks May 19th annually as Abhakara Memorial Day, and the shrine sees increased activity around this date with official ceremonies and visits from naval personnel. Local fishermen and sailors regularly pay respects at the shrine before heading out to sea, and the surrounding grounds are kept immaculately maintained by the navy.

## Practical Information

The shrine is open daily from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM with free admission. The hilltop location offers excellent views, and the walk up from the parking area takes about 10 minutes along a paved path. Combine your visit with time at Sapli Beach directly below, which offers clear water and a relaxed atmosphere. The shrine is located about 20 kilometers north of Chumphon town and is easily reached by car or motorbike.
description.nl: Een monument en heiligdom op een heuveltop met uitzicht over Sapli Beach, gewijd aan Prins Abhakara Kiartivongse, bekend als de Vader van de Koninklijke Thaise Marine. De prins wordt diep vereerd in de provincie Chumphon. Het heiligdom heeft een standbeeld, marinmuseum en panoramisch uitzicht over de kustlijn van de Golf van Thailand.

best_time_to_visit: Morning for comfortable temperatures; May 19th for the annual Abhakara Memorial Day ceremony
tips: Walk up to the hilltop shrine first for the panoramic views then head down to Sapli Beach for swimming
Visit the small naval museum inside the complex for interesting historical context about the Royal Thai Navy
The shrine base is shaped like a naval ship which makes for a unique photograph
